title: Workflow Definition v1
description: Language-neutral contract for a workflow/usecase definition, shared by the Python doc-workflows engine (qte77/azure-doc-workflows, pydantic Workflow/Stage) and the TypeScript Cloudflare Worker engine (qte77/ldnmxx-hack, UsecaseDef/StageDef). `id` and `stages[].name` are the only fields both engines must agree on; every other field is documented per-engine below and type-checked when present, but unrecognized keys are allowed so each engine can carry its own extras.

id.description: Unique workflow/usecase identifier. Registry key for both engines (Python Workflow.id, TS UsecaseDef.id).

title.description: Human-readable display name. TS engine field (UsecaseDef.title, required there). Python engine's equivalent is `label`.

label.description: Human-readable display name / docx-title override. Python engine field (Workflow.label, defaults to ""). TS engine's equivalent is `title`.

kind.description: Workflow-level pipeline family. Python engine only (Workflow.kind, e.g. "text" | "image"; default "text"). TS usecases do not set this at the top level — TS's `kind` lives per-stage (see $defs/stage).

stages.description: Ordered execution plan. Array order is significant: both engines execute stages in the order given here.

$comment: additionalProperties is deliberately true at the envelope level: the Python model carries further fields this contract doesn't standardize yet (order, triage_hint, template, standards). Only fields listed in `properties` are type-checked; anything else passes through unchecked.

stage.description: One step in stages[]. `name` is the only field required by this contract; everything else is engine-specific and optional here even where one engine's own (stricter) runtime guard requires it locally.

name.description: Stage identifier, unique within stages[]. This is the reconciled field name: the TS engine's source field was `span`, renamed to `name` to align with the Python engine's Stage.name for this contract.

kind.description: Stage category. TS engine field (StageDef.kind, required there, e.g. "plan" | "tool"). Python engine does not use a per-stage kind (its `kind` is workflow-level, see top-level `kind`).

backend.description: Python engine only (Stage.backend). Extraction backend for this stage, e.g. document_intelligence | gpt_vision | openpyxl | docx | phash.

model.description: Python engine only (Stage.model). LLM/VLM model id for a generate/classify stage, e.g. gpt-5, gpt-4o-mini.

cache_prefix.description: Python engine only (Stage.cache_prefix). Ordered list of inputs forming the cached prompt prefix (azure-doc-workflows ADR-0006).

rubric.description: Python engine only (Stage.rubric). Path to the evaluation standard/rubric document for an evaluate stage.

judge.description: Python engine only (Stage.judge). Judge model/identifier for an evaluate stage.

events.description: TS engine only (StageDef.events). Canned AG-UI/A2UI events replayed verbatim; used as-is when the stage has no `exec`, or as the fallback when a keyed `exec` dispatch misses.

exec.description: TS engine only (StageDef.exec). Name of the forced tool/model call this stage dispatches to on the free-chain path (e.g. assess_stage, search_opportunities). Absent means the stage's canned `events` always play as-is.

stage.$comment: additionalProperties is deliberately true here too, for the same forward-compatibility reason as the envelope: documented keys above are type-checked when present, undocumented keys pass through unchecked.

systemInstruction.description: Per-archetype task instruction for a generate stage. Python engine only (Stage.system, azure-doc-workflows ADR-0004). Either a single language-agnostic string, or an object keyed by language code (e.g. {"en": "...", "de": "..."}) for per-language system prompts (azure-doc-workflows#167).

agentEvent.description: TS engine only (AgentEvent). One AG-UI/A2UI protocol event replayed verbatim by a canned stage.

type.description: AG-UI/A2UI event type, e.g. STEP_STARTED, TEXT_MESSAGE_CONTENT, TOOL_CALL_START, TOOL_CALL_END.

a2uiMessages.description: Opaque A2UI card-batch payload; not further constrained by this contract.

output.description: Where/how the workflow's result surfaces. oneOf the Python doc-workflows shape or the TS worker shape - an engine only ever emits its own shape, so the two are mutually exclusive here.

required_sections.description: Layer-1 structural gate (azure-doc-workflows ADR-0005). Either a flat language-agnostic list, or a {lang: [...]} map of per-output-language required section names.

mode.description: Names the code path (RenderMode) that builds the final A2UI card batch, e.g. founders | route.
